message:
test(fp-conformance): live oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling std|var (br-frankenpandas-fmmq)

Extends the rolling-family live-oracle harness landed in de68f8b +
3771fd1 with ddof=1 std() and var() coverage, matching the
fp-frame apply_grouped_rolling(.std()/.var()) surface that has been
available since commit 59374a2.

crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:
- Adds FixtureOperation::DataFrameGroupByRollingStd and ...Var
  enum variants with serde rename/alias for the canonical and
  data_frame_* spellings.
- Threads both through all 10 match sites (operation_name, the
  strict-mode dispatcher, compat contract map, expected-payload
  resolution, run_fixture_operation, execute_dataframe_groupby_rolling_fixture_operation,
  and the oracle-evidence match in the conformance gate).
- execute_dataframe_groupby_rolling_fixture_operation dispatches
  .std() / .var() on GroupByRolling.
- Two new live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_{std,var}_matches_pandas
  tests using 8-row interleaved a/b groups and a Null mid-window
  to exercise the ddof=1 min-periods=1 path.

crates/fp-conformance/oracle/pandas_oracle.py:
- op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_std/var thin wrappers that forward
  to op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, "std"/"var", ...).
- dispatch learns the canonical + data_frame_* alias spellings.

Tests (rch): live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_{mean,sum,min,max,count,std,var}_matches_pandas 7/7 pass. No changes to the rolling executor on the fp-frame side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3195,6 +3195,18 @@ def op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str,
     )
 
 
+def op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_std(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(
+        pd, payload, "std", "dataframe_groupby_rolling_std"
+    )
+
+
+def op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_var(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(
+        pd, payload, "var", "dataframe_groupby_rolling_var"
+    )
+
+
 def op_dataframe_groupby_cumcount(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
     groupby_columns = payload.get("groupby_columns")
@@ -4622,6 +4634,10 @@ def dispatch(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_max(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_count", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_count"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_std", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_std"}:
+        return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_std(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_var", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_var"}:
+        return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_var(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_cumcount", "data_frame_groupby_cumcount"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_cumcount(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_ngroup", "data_frame_groupby_ngroup"}:
